author: @jeremyconley9061
text: I still come back here to reminisce. Hope you are doing well Rob!

author: @samuraikitty9207
text: Hi Robert, is this completely safe to do? My father thinks that if I do this and come in contact with any of the chemicals it'll affect me in the long run. And that the sulphuric acid can be dangerous, too. But that's what you gotta do when you want to experiment lol.

author: @johnmcfadden9336
text: now it appears Graphene Oxide Man has  his side kick Go Guy Go . i guess ill i have to be content with being one of the nerd herd gang.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - he was only visiting mate - just part of my open lab - anytime you want to come down you are welcome

replies.author: @astrialkil
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith Careful with that one..5000 people might show up one day.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: astrialkil lol - now that has me nervous - not enough tea bags - better buy a few

author: @ClownWhisper
text: never seen nitrate salts added...  either that or i've gone senile

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - it's hummer's mate better is tour which uses phosphoric acid

author: @TZJHandle
text: Could you advise me where to get peroxide from... i've been all over to try and find some, not been able to locally.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's used as contact lens cleaner and for cuts and burns - and some places dyeing hair - dentists use it for bleaching teeth - the best place is a pharmacy - you can get 3% solution - which is what is needed

replies.author: @TZJHandle
replies.text: I'll have to have another look

author: @mkhamlach
text:
Hi. What is the concentration of sulfuric acid?
Go Go Go Jeremy make GO.

author: @juliec9307
text:
I really appreciate naming the sources of the materialson the video. It  makes a Huge Difference psychologically for a beginner. It can make the difference between "how can i acquire" to "YES I CAN" 
Thanks again for all these video. I am inspired to Take action!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that's really cool do a vid - i'd love to see

author: @actudoran
text:
good show Rob - as always a good sport :) letting friends play with your toys takes quite some unearthly detachment :) - I always cringe when friends want to help me do stuff ... be it on the motorbike or on "creative" stuff ... 
great job Jeremy !

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - i was cringing inside mate

replies.author: @actudoran
replies.text:
ok Rob, I'm sorry to ask you this question here but here goes:
could graphene say 5% in water be used for a magnetohydrodynamic convertor at lower temperatures ? say 150 to 300 degrees C ?
effectively you are seeding the fluid with graphene ... passing it through a magnetic field ...  should be able to get some electricity out...

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
actudoran
do you know - i have no idea - but it wouldn't take much to try it - so i will - especially as a friend just sent me some stuff on JP Petit and MHD

replies.author: @actudoran
replies.text: Wow cool! Got to feed the baby and will get back to you in a short while

author: @DanielbenDaniel
text: Nice! Hey Jeremy are you a plastics engineer?

Dan Murphy
